	sublineb = parent.Graph.par.Sublinecolorxb.eval()
	sublinea = parent.Graph.par.Sublinecolorxa.eval()
	tickwidth = parent.Graph.par.Lineswidthx.eval()

	units = parent.Graph.par.Unitsx.eval() 
	logticks = (units in ['log10', 'log2', 'audiohz']) and parent.Graph.par.Logticksx == 'constantstep'

	dataw = ipar.Graph.Datarangex2 - ipar.Graph.Datarangex1
	dataw = max(.0001, dataw) 


	if parent.Graph.par.Lineadaptivex :
		pixelw = parent.Graph.width
		numlabelsep = 82
		numlabels = int(pixelw / numlabelsep)
		datasep = dataw / numlabels
		oom = int(math.log10(datasep))
		normdatasep = datasep / 10**oom
		if normdatasep < 1. :
			gooddatasep = 1. 
		elif normdatasep < 2. :
			gooddatasep = 2. 
		elif normdatasep < 5. :
			gooddatasep = 5. 
		else:
			gooddatasep = 10. 
		#print(numlabels, datasep, oom, normdatasep, gooddatasep)
		if logticks :

		else:
			tickintervals = 10 if (gooddatasep==1.) else (8 if (gooddatasep==2.) else (10 if (gooddatasep==5.) else 10))		
		
		gooddatasep = gooddatasep * 10**oom
		linestart = gooddatasep * (1 + int(ipar.Graph.Datarangex1/gooddatasep))

		elif units in ['log10', 'log2', 'audiohz'] :
			numintervals = round(ipar.Graph.Datarangex2 - ipar.Graph.Datarangex1)			
		elif units == 'decibels' :
			steps = [3, 10][parent.Graph.par.Decibelstepsx == '10dB']
			numintervals = round((ipar.Graph.Datarangex2 - ipar.Graph.Datarangex1) / steps)
	
		ipar.Graph.Numintervalsx = numintervals			
		
		gooddatasep = dataw / float(numintervals)
		tickintervals = parent.Graph.par.Tickintervalsx.eval()
		linestart = textstart = ipar.Graph.Datarangex1

	x0, y0 = dataToGraph(0., 0.)
	x1, y1 = dataToGraph(gooddatasep, 0.)
	dx = x1 - x0

	dtick = dx / tickintervals

	ticklist = []

	if (units == 'log10' or units == 'audiohz') and logticks:
		for i in range(0, tickintervals) :
			ticklist = ticklist + [dx * math.log10(1+9*i/tickintervals)]
		uniformticks = False
		
	elif (units == 'log2') and logticks:
		for i in range(0, tickintervals) :
			ticklist = ticklist + [dx * math.log2(1+1*i/tickintervals)]
		uniformticks = False
		
	else: 
		for i in range(0, tickintervals) :
			ticklist = ticklist + [i*dtick]
		uniformticks = True
				
	#print(ticklist)
	
	line = linestart - gooddatasep

	px0, py0 = dataToGraph(line, ipar.Graph.Datarangey1.eval())
	px1, py1 = dataToGraph(line, ipar.Graph.Datarangey2.eval())
	
	if parent.Graph.par.Linesx :
		poly = scriptOp.appendPoly(2, closed=False)
		p = poly[0].point
		p.x, p.y = px0, py0

		p = poly[1].point
		p.x, p.y = px1, py1
	
	if parent.Graph.par.Ticksx :
		for i in range(-(tickintervals), 0) :
			x = px0 + ticklist[i%tickintervals] - (dx if i<0 else 0)
			tickop.appendRow([x, py0, tickz, tickr, tickg, tickb, ticka, tickwidth])
			tickop.appendRow([x, py0 + parent.Graph.par.Tickslenx, tickz, tickr, tickg, tickb, ticka, tickwidth])
			tickop.appendRow([x, py0 + parent.Graph.par.Tickslenx, tickz, subliner, sublineg, sublineb, sublinea, tickwidth])
			tickop.appendRow([x, parent.Graph.par.Graphrangey2, tickz, subliner, sublineg, sublineb, sublinea, tickwidth])
			
			if uniformticks and i == -tickintervals :
				break


	linecopies = int((ipar.Graph.Datarangex2 - linestart) / gooddatasep)
	op('copylinesx').par.ncy = linecopies + 2
	op('copylinesx').par.tx = dx
	op('copyticksx').par.ncy = (linecopies + 3) * (tickintervals if uniformticks else 1)
	op('copyticksx').par.tx = dx / (tickintervals if uniformticks else 1)

	line = textstart
	textend = (ipar.Graph.Datarangex2 + gooddatasep*parent.Graph.par.Lineadaptivex*.33 + .001)
	while line <= textend :
		px, py = dataToGraph(line, 0)
		format = '%.' + str(parent.Graph.par.Decimalplacesx if (parent.Graph.par.Unitsx=='linear') else 0) + 'f'
		text = format % line
		labelsop.appendRow([text, px])	

		line = line + gooddatasep

	return

def dataToGraph(x,y):
	normx = (x - ipar.Graph.Datarangex1) / (ipar.Graph.Datarangex2 - ipar.Graph.Datarangex1)
	normy = (y - ipar.Graph.Datarangey1) / (ipar.Graph.Datarangey2 - ipar.Graph.Datarangey1) 

	gx = ipar.Graph.Graphx1 + normx * (ipar.Graph.Graphx2 - ipar.Graph.Graphx1)
	gy = ipar.Graph.Graphy1 + normy * (ipar.Graph.Graphy2 - ipar.Graph.Graphy1)

	return gx, gy

TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions

class ButtonExt(SubWidgetExt):
	"""
	ButtonExt provides all functionality for basic widget button
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.parPrefix = 'Button'
		SubWidgetExt.__init__(self, ownerComp)
		self.valueIndex = self.ownerComp.digits

		self.types = [
				('Momentary', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.SetValue(1),
							   'onToOff': lambda p: self.SetValue(0)}),
				('Momentary Up', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.SetValue(0),
								  'onToOff': lambda p: self.SetValue(1)}),
				('Toggle Down', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.Toggle()}),
				('Toggle Up',
				 		{'onToOff': lambda p: self.Toggle(checkInside=True)}),
				('Toggle Up Anywhere', {'onToOff':lambda p: self.Toggle()}),
				('Radio Down', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Radio Up',
				 		{'onToOff': lambda p: self.Radio(checkInside=True)}),
				('Radio Up Anywhere', {'onToOff':lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Exclusive Down', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Exclusive Up',
				 		{'onToOff': lambda p: self.Radio(checkInside=True)}),
				('Exclusive Up Anywhere', {'onToOff':lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Commands Only', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.RunOnCommand(),
							   	   'onToOff': lambda p: self.RunOffCommand()}),
				('Commands Only Ignore Value', {
								   'offToOn': lambda p: self.RunOnCommand(),
							   	   'onToOff': lambda p: self.RunOffCommand()})
		]
		self.buttonTypePar = parent.Widget.par.Buttontype
		self.ownerComp.par.Buttontype.menuLabels = [type[0] for type in self.types]
		self.ownerComp.par.Buttontype.menuNames = self.ownerComp.par.Buttontype.menuLabels

	def SetValue(self, value):
		"""
		Sets the value of the widget
		"""
		setattr(self.ownerComp.parent.Widget.par,
										'Value'+str(self.valueIndex), value)

	def GetValue(self):
		"""
		Sets the value of the widget
		"""
		#debug('value change')
		return getattr(self.ownerComp.parent.Widget.par,
										'Value'+str(self.valueIndex))

	def Radio(self, checkInside=False):
		if self.Toggle(checkInside):
			self.updateRadioGroup()

	def Toggle(self, checkInside=False):
		"""
		Sets the value of the button to on when off, and off when on

		:param checkInside: if True, only toggle if the mouse is currently
			inside the ownerComp panel.
		:return: False if skipped from not inside, otherwise True
		"""
		if checkInside and not self.ownerComp.panel.inside:
			return False
		self.SetValue(1-self.GetValue())
		return True

	def RunOnCommand(self):
		"""
		Runs the python command for when the button turns on
		"""
		#debug('command on')
		command = parent.Widget.par.Offtoonscript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)
		command = parent.Widget.par.Onvaluechangescript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)

	def RunOffCommand(self):
		"""
		Runs the python command for when the button turns off
		"""
		#debug('command off')
		command = parent.Widget.par.Ontooffscript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)
		command = parent.Widget.par.Onvaluechangescript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)
	@property
	def GroupButtons(self):
		table = parent.Widget.par.Buttongroupdat.eval()
		if not isinstance(table, DAT):
			return []
		tableParent = table.parent()
		return [tableParent.op(c.val)
							for c in table.col(0) if tableParent.op(c.val)]

	def onPanelEvent(self, event, panelValue=None):
		"""
		Selects correct funtion based on button widget type
		"""
		#debug('panelEvent', event)
		typeIndex = self.buttonTypePar.menuIndex
		btype = self.types[typeIndex][1].get(event)
		#debug(self.types[typeIndex][0])
		if btype:
			#debug('onPanel', event)
			btype(panelValue)

	def onParValueChange(self, par, prev):
		"""
		The Button's value has changed.
		Only runs when necessary (see parexecValue 'Active' parameter).
		"""
		type = self.buttonTypePar.eval()
		if type == 'Commands_Only_Ignore_Value':
			return
		if type.startswith('Radio') or type.startswith('Exclusive'):
			self.updateRadioGroup()
		# if par.eval():
		# 	self.RunOnCommand()
		# else:
		# 	self.RunOffCommand()

	def updateRadioGroup(self):
		"""Turn off other radio buttons

		:return: False if change reverted because trying to turn off Radio,
			otherwise True
		"""
		#debug('radiogroup')
		type = self.buttonTypePar.eval()
		groupButtons = self.GroupButtons
		if type.startswith('Radio') and parent.Widget.par.Value0 == 0 and \
				not any([button.par.Value0 for button in groupButtons]):
			self.SetValue(1)
			return False
		if parent.Widget.par.Value0:
			for button in groupButtons:
				if button != parent.Widget:
					button.par.Value0 = False
		return True

	def onParSettingsChange(self, par, prev):
		if par.name == self.buttonTypePar.name:
			if par.eval().startswith('Momentary'):
				self.onPanelEvent('onToOff')
			elif par.eval().startswith('Radio') \
									or par.eval().startswith('Exclusive'):
				self.updateRadioGroup()
			parent.Widget.par.Buttongroupdat.enable = \
										par.eval().startswith('Radio') \
										or par.eval().startswith('Exclusive')
		else:
			SubWidgetExt.onParSettingsChange(self, par, prev)
